key_insight: A physics-informed SVC trained on sparse experimental data suggests that moderate Ti, Ni, and Co additions can improve room-temperature ductility in tungsten-based RHEAs, while high Cr content tends to increase brittleness.
engineering_relevance: These rules provide an interpretable screening guide for designing tougher tungsten-based alloys for fusion-relevant service conditions.
method: Curated experimental dataset of tungsten-containing alloys was compiled.
Composition-based, physics-informed descriptors were used for machine learning.
Three classifiers were tested with nested cross-validation; SVC performed best and SHAP identified key drivers.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports that SVC had the best generalization in nested cross-validation, with predictions aligning with published experimental trends and DFT-derived elastic descriptors.
watch_item: The mechanical dataset is sparse and heterogeneous, so predictions should be treated as screening guidance rather than direct design guarantees.
paper_title: Ductility Design Rules for Tungsten based Refractory High Entropy Alloys from Sparse Experimental Datasets
